---
id: "MP142"
title: "Configuration-Driven Pipeline"
type: "meta-principle"
category: "data_management"
date_created: "2025-12-23"
date_modified: "2025-12-30"
author: "Claude"
status: "active"
version: "1.1"
weight: 142
priority: "HIGH"
related_to:
  - "MP140": "Pipeline Orchestration Principle - On-demand execution model"
  - "MP141": "Scheduled Execution Pattern - Scheduling and observability"
  - "MP108": "ETL Phase Sequence Principle - 6-layer phase ordering"
  - "MP064": "ETL-Derivation Separation - Directory structure"
  - "MP065": "Platform Configuration Management - Platform catalog authority"
  - "DF00": "Pipeline Architecture - 6-layer symmetric data flow"
  - "SO_P016": "Configuration Scope Hierarchy - Three-layer config architecture"
  - "MP122": "Triple-Track Subrepo Architecture - Config at Track 3"
implements:
  - "SINGLE_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH": "One configuration file for pipeline definition"
  - "DECLARATIVE_CONFIGURATION": "YAML-based pipeline specification"
  - "AUTO_DISCOVERY": "Script scanning and metadata extraction"
  - "VERSION_CONTROLLED": "Configuration tracked in git"
  - "THREE_LAYER_CONFIG": "Universal template + Company override + Generated output"
---

# MP142: Configuration-Driven Pipeline

## Core Statement

**All pipeline definitions MUST be centralized in a single configuration file using standardized YAML format, supporting multi-platform and multi-layer (ETL/DRV) orchestration with automatic script discovery and explicit dependency specification.**

### The Golden Rule of Configuration

> **"The configuration file is the single source of truth for what pipelines exist, how they relate, and in what order they execute."**

## Three-Layer Configuration Architecture (SO_P016)

Per **SO_P016 Configuration Scope Hierarchy** and **MP122 Triple-Track Subrepo**, pipeline configuration follows a three-layer architecture:

```
Configuration Layer Architecture
================================

Layer 1: Universal (Base Template)
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ global_scripts/templates/_targets_config.base.yaml          │
│ - Schema definitions                                        │
│ - Phase ordering (6-layer: 0IM→5NM)                        │
│ - Default execution settings                                │
│ - Excluded patterns                                         │
│ ✗ NO company-specific settings                              │
│ 📦 Shared via git subrepo (Track 1)                         │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              ↓ merge
Layer 2: Company (Override)
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ app_config.yaml > pipeline: section                         │
│ - Platform enabled flags                                    │
│ - DRV groups selection                                      │
│ - Execution overrides (workers, timeout)                    │
│ - Scheduling configuration                                  │
│ 📁 At project root (Track 3)                                │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              ↓ generates
Layer 3: Generated (Final Config)
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ {project_root}/_targets_config.yaml                         │
│ - Complete merged configuration                             │
│ - Ready for targets execution                               │
│ - Auto-generated, version controlled                        │
│ 📁 At project root (Track 3)                                │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Why Project Root?

Since `update_scripts/` is also a **git subrepo** (shared across projects), the generated `_targets_config.yaml` MUST be at the **project root** (Company scope), not inside the subrepo.

```
{project_root}/                          ← Company scope (Track 3)
├── app_config.yaml                      ← Company settings
├── _targets_config.yaml                 ← Generated config (HERE!)
└── scripts/
    ├── global_scripts/                  ← git subrepo #1 (Track 1)
    │   └── templates/
    │       └── _targets_config.base.yaml
    └── update_scripts/                  ← git subrepo #2 (Track 2)
        ├── _targets.R                   ← Reads ../../_targets_config.yaml
        └── Makefile                     ← CLI entry point
```

## Configuration File Standard

### Location and Naming

```yaml
configuration_standard:
  # Generated output (Company scope - Track 3)
  location: "{project_root}/_targets_config.yaml"

  # Base template (Universal scope - Track 1)
  base_template: "global_scripts/templates/_targets_config.base.yaml"

  # Company override source
  override_source: "app_config.yaml > pipeline:"

  format: "YAML 1.2"
  encoding: "UTF-8"
  version_controlled: true
```

### Top-Level Structure

```yaml
# _targets_config.yaml

# Pipeline metadata
version: "1.0"
last_updated: "2025-12-23"

# 6-layer phase ordering (from DF00)
phase_order:
  - "0IM"   # Import (ETL)
  - "1ST"   # Staging (ETL)
  - "2TR"   # Transform (ETL)
  - "3PR"   # Process (DRV)
  - "4CL"   # Cleanse (DRV)
  - "5NM"   # Normalize (DRV)

# Platform definitions
platforms:
  cbz:
    name: "Cyberbiz"
    drv_groups: ["D01", "D04"]

  amz:
    name: "Amazon"
    drv_groups: ["D01", "D04"]

  eby:
    name: "eBay"
    drv_groups: ["D01", "D04"]

  all:
    name: "Cross-Platform"
    depends_on_platforms: ["cbz", "amz", "eby"]
    drv_groups: ["D04"]

# Auto-discovery settings
discovery:
  etl_pattern: "{platform}_ETL_{datatype}_{phase}.R"
  drv_pattern: "{platform}_D{group}_{seq}.R"
  scan_directories:
    etl: "ETL/{platform}/"
    drv: "DRV/{platform}/"
```

### Company Override in app_config.yaml

The `pipeline:` section in `app_config.yaml` provides company-specific overrides:

```yaml
# app_config.yaml (Company Scope)
pipeline:
  # Enable/disable platforms for this company
  platforms:
    cbz:
      enabled: true
      drv_groups: ["D01", "D04"]
    amz:
      enabled: true
      drv_groups: ["D01", "D03"]
    eby:
      enabled: true
      drv_groups: ["D01", "D04"]
    all:
      enabled: true
      depends_on_platforms: ["cbz", "amz", "eby"]
    precision:
      enabled: true

  # Override default execution settings
  execution:
    parallel_workers: 2  # Conservative for this server

  # Scheduling configuration
  scheduling:
    mode: "manual"  # "manual" or "scheduled"
    schedule: "0 2 * * 0"  # Sunday 2:00 AM (if scheduled)
```

### Project Execution Scope with Shared Scripts

`update_scripts/` is a shared subrepo across projects (MP122). The pipeline configuration defines the project execution scope; platform directories and scripts remain part of the global inventory even when disabled for a specific project. **Never delete or archive platform scripts to control scope.** Scope is controlled in `app_config.yaml` and the generated `_targets_config.yaml`.

```yaml
# app_config.yaml (Company scope)
pipeline:
  platforms:
    cbz: { enabled: true }
    eby: { enabled: true }
    amz: { enabled: false } # Present in inventory, disabled for this project
```

## Configuration Merge Function

### fn_merge_pipeline_config.R

The merge function combines base template with company overrides:

```r
# global_scripts/04_utils/fn_merge_pipeline_config.R
merge_pipeline_config <- function(
  base_path = NULL,
  app_config_path = NULL,
  output_path = NULL,
  scan_scripts = FALSE,
  verbose = TRUE
) {
  # Default paths (relative to update_scripts/)
  if (is.null(base_path)) {
    base_path <- file.path("..", "global_scripts", "templates",
                           "_targets_config.base.yaml")
  }
  if (is.null(app_config_path)) {
    app_config_path <- file.path("..", "..", "app_config.yaml")
  }
  if (is.null(output_path)) {
    output_path <- file.path("..", "..", "_targets_config.yaml")
  }

  # 1. Read base template (Universal scope)
  base <- yaml::read_yaml(base_path)

  # 2. Read company override (from app_config.yaml pipeline: section)
  app_config <- yaml::read_yaml(app_config_path)
  override <- app_config$pipeline %||% list()

  # 3. Deep merge (override wins)
  merged <- modifyList(base, override)

  # 4. Update metadata
  merged$last_updated <- format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d")
  merged$template_type <- "merged"

  # 5. Write output
  yaml::write_yaml(merged, output_path)

  message("✓ Pipeline config merged: ", output_path)
  invisible(merged)
}
```

### Makefile Configuration Commands

```makefile
# In update_scripts/Makefile
# Per SO_P016: Configuration Scope Hierarchy

config-merge:
	@echo "Merging pipeline configuration..."
	@$(R) -e "source('$(GLOBAL_SCRIPTS)/04_utils/fn_merge_pipeline_config.R'); \
	  merge_pipeline_config()"

config-scan:
	@echo "Scanning ETL/DRV directories and updating script lists..."
	@$(R) -e "source('$(GLOBAL_SCRIPTS)/04_utils/fn_merge_pipeline_config.R'); \
	  config <- yaml::read_yaml('$(CONFIG_PATH)'); \
	  config <- scan_and_update_scripts(config); \
	  yaml::write_yaml(config, '$(CONFIG_PATH)')"

config-full:
	@echo "Full configuration: merge + scan + validate"
	$(MAKE) config-merge
	$(MAKE) config-scan
	$(MAKE) config-validate

config-validate:
	@echo "Validating $(CONFIG_PATH)..."
	@$(R) -e "config <- yaml::read_yaml('$(CONFIG_PATH)'); \
	  cat('✓ Platforms:', paste(names(config\$$platforms), collapse=', '))"
```

### Configuration Workflow

```
Developer Workflow:

1. Modify app_config.yaml pipeline: section
   ↓
2. make config-merge    # Merge base + override
   ↓
3. make config-scan     # Scan actual scripts, update dependencies
   ↓
4. make config-validate # Validate configuration
   ↓
5. make run             # Execute pipeline
```

## Auto-Discovery Logic

### ETL Script Discovery

The system automatically discovers ETL scripts based on directory structure and naming patterns:

```yaml
etl_discovery:
  scan_pattern: "ETL/{platform}/*.R"

  filename_parsing:
    example: "cbz_ETL_sales_2TR.R"
    extracted:
      platform: "cbz"
      datatype: "sales"
      phase: "2TR"

  auto_dependencies:
    rule: "Same datatype, earlier phase"
    example: |
      cbz_ETL_sales_1ST depends on cbz_ETL_sales_0IM
      cbz_ETL_sales_2TR depends on cbz_ETL_sales_1ST
```

### DRV Script Discovery

```yaml
drv_discovery:
  scan_pattern: "DRV/{platform}/*.R"

  filename_parsing:
    example: "cbz_D04_02.R"
    extracted:
      platform: "cbz"
      group: "D04"
      sequence: "02"

  auto_dependencies:
    within_group: "Earlier sequence number"
    cross_group: "From DEPENDS_ON_DRV markers"
```

### Marker Extraction

During discovery, the system reads script headers for explicit dependencies:

```yaml
marker_extraction:
  scan_lines: "First 20 lines of each script"

  markers_extracted:
    - "CONSUMES"
    - "PRODUCES"
    - "DEPENDS_ON_ETL"
    - "DEPENDS_ON_DRV"

  override_behavior: "Explicit markers override auto-discovery"
```

## Explicit Dependency Configuration

### When Auto-Discovery is Insufficient

Some dependencies cannot be inferred from naming alone. These MUST be explicitly configured:

```yaml
# Explicit dependency overrides
explicit_dependencies:
  drv:
    cbz_D04_02:
      depends_on_etl:
        - "cbz_ETL_sales_2TR"
      depends_on_drv:
        - "cbz_D04_01"
      note: "D04_02 needs sales data and D04_01 output"

    all_D04_08:
      depends_on_drv:
        - "cbz_D04_02"
        - "eby_D04_02"
        - "amz_D04_02"
      note: "Cross-platform summary requires all platform outputs"
```

### ETL Cross-Datatype Dependencies (Rare)

```yaml
# Rare case: ETL depends on another ETL's datatype
etl_special_dependencies:
  cbz_ETL_orders_2TR:
    depends_on_etl:
      - "cbz_ETL_customers_2TR"
    note: "Orders JOIN requires customer reference data"
```

## Generated Configuration Example

### Complete _targets_config.yaml

```yaml
# _targets_config.yaml
# Auto-generated + manually verified
# Last scan: 2025-12-23T14:30:00

version: "1.0"
last_updated: "2025-12-23"

phase_order: ["0IM", "1ST", "2TR", "3PR", "4CL", "5NM"]

platforms:
  cbz:
    name: "Cyberbiz"
    drv_groups: ["D01", "D04"]

    etl:
      # Auto-discovered ETL pipelines
      customers:
        phases: ["0IM", "1ST", "2TR"]
        scripts:
          - { script: "cbz_ETL_customers_0IM.R", phase: "0IM" }
          - { script: "cbz_ETL_customers_1ST.R", phase: "1ST", depends: ["cbz_ETL_customers_0IM"] }
          - { script: "cbz_ETL_customers_2TR.R", phase: "2TR", depends: ["cbz_ETL_customers_1ST"] }

      orders:
        phases: ["0IM", "1ST", "2TR"]
        scripts:
          - { script: "cbz_ETL_orders_0IM.R", phase: "0IM" }
          - { script: "cbz_ETL_orders_1ST.R", phase: "1ST", depends: ["cbz_ETL_orders_0IM"] }
          - { script: "cbz_ETL_orders_2TR.R", phase: "2TR", depends: ["cbz_ETL_orders_1ST"] }

      sales:
        phases: ["0IM", "1ST", "2TR"]
        scripts:
          - { script: "cbz_ETL_sales_0IM.R", phase: "0IM" }
          - { script: "cbz_ETL_sales_1ST.R", phase: "1ST", depends: ["cbz_ETL_sales_0IM"] }
          - { script: "cbz_ETL_sales_2TR.R", phase: "2TR", depends: ["cbz_ETL_sales_1ST"] }

    drv:
      # Auto-discovered DRV scripts with explicit dependencies
      D01:
        - { script: "cbz_D01_03.R", depends_etl: ["cbz_ETL_customers_2TR", "cbz_ETL_orders_2TR"] }
        - { script: "cbz_D01_04.R", depends_drv: ["cbz_D01_03"] }
        - { script: "cbz_D01_05.R", depends_drv: ["cbz_D01_04"] }
        - { script: "cbz_D01_06.R", depends_drv: ["cbz_D01_05"] }
        - { script: "cbz_D01_07.R", depends_drv: ["cbz_D01_06"] }

      D04:
        - { script: "cbz_D04_01.R", depends_etl: ["cbz_ETL_sales_2TR"] }
        - { script: "cbz_D04_02.R", depends_drv: ["cbz_D04_01"] }
        - { script: "cbz_D04_03.R", depends_drv: ["cbz_D04_02"] }
        - { script: "cbz_D04_04.R", depends_drv: ["cbz_D04_03"] }
        - { script: "cbz_D04_05.R", depends_drv: ["cbz_D04_04"] }
        - { script: "cbz_D04_06.R", depends_drv: ["cbz_D04_05"] }

  all:
    name: "Cross-Platform"
    depends_on_platforms: ["cbz", "amz", "eby"]

    drv:
      D04:
        - { script: "all_D04_08.R", depends_drv: ["cbz_D04_02", "amz_D04_02", "eby_D04_02"] }
```

## Configuration Generator

### Scan and Generate Script

```r
# R script to generate _targets_config.yaml from directory scan
generate_targets_config <- function(base_path = "update_scripts") {
  config <- list(
    version = "1.0",
    last_updated = format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d"),
    # 6-Layer Symmetric Pipeline (revised 2025-12-24)
    phase_order = c("0IM", "1ST", "2TR", "3PR", "4CL", "5NM"),
    platforms = list()
  )

  platforms <- c("cbz", "amz", "eby", "all")

  for (platform in platforms) {
    config$platforms[[platform]] <- list(
      name = get_platform_name(platform),
      etl = scan_etl_scripts(base_path, platform),
      drv = scan_drv_scripts(base_path, platform)
    )
  }

  yaml::write_yaml(config, file.path(base_path, "_targets_config.yaml"))
  message("Generated _targets_config.yaml")
}

scan_etl_scripts <- function(base_path, platform) {
  etl_dir <- file.path(base_path, "ETL", platform)
  if (!dir.exists(etl_dir)) return(list())

  scripts <- list.files(etl_dir, pattern = "\\.R$")
  # Parse and organize by datatype and phase...
}

scan_drv_scripts <- function(base_path, platform) {
  drv_dir <- file.path(base_path, "DRV", platform)
  if (!dir.exists(drv_dir)) return(list())

  scripts <- list.files(drv_dir, pattern = "\\.R$")
  # Parse and extract dependency markers...
}
```

### CLI Command

```bash
# Generate configuration from directory scan
make config-generate

# Validate configuration against actual scripts
make config-validate

# Show differences between config and actual state
make config-diff
```

## Configuration Validation

### Required Validations

```yaml
validation_rules:
  structural:
    - "All referenced scripts must exist"
    - "All dependencies must be defined"
    - "No circular dependencies"
    - "Phase order is valid"

  naming:
    - "Script names follow naming conventions"
    - "Platforms match directory structure"
    - "Groups follow DM_R042 numbering"

  completeness:
    - "All scripts in directories are in config"
    - "No orphan entries (config without script)"
    - "Marker dependencies match config"
```

### Validation Output

```bash
$ make config-validate

Validating _targets_config.yaml...

✓ All 47 referenced scripts exist
✓ All dependencies are defined
✓ No circular dependencies detected
✓ Phase order is valid

⚠ Warning: 2 scripts not in config:
  - ETL/cbz/cbz_ETL_reviews_0IM.R (new?)
  - DRV/cbz/cbz_D04_07.R (new?)

Run 'make config-generate' to add new scripts.
```

## Configuration Updates

### When Configuration Must Update

```yaml
update_triggers:
  automatic:
    - "New script added to ETL/ or DRV/"
    - "Script renamed or removed"
    - "New platform directory created"

  manual:
    - "Dependency markers changed in script"
    - "Cross-platform dependencies added"
    - "Special ETL cross-datatype dependency"
```

### Update Workflow

```bash
# 1. Make code changes (add/modify scripts)

# 2. Regenerate config to capture changes
make config-generate

# 3. Review and verify changes
git diff _targets_config.yaml

# 4. Validate the updated config
make config-validate

# 5. Commit config with code changes
git add _targets_config.yaml
git commit -m "Update pipeline configuration"
```

## Integration with targets Package

### Reading Configuration

```r
# In _targets.R
library(targets)
library(yaml)

# Load configuration
config <- yaml::read_yaml("_targets_config.yaml")

# Get selected platform from environment
platform <- Sys.getenv("MAMBA_PLATFORM", "all")

# Build targets from configuration
build_targets_from_config <- function(config, platform) {
  targets_list <- list()

  # Add ETL targets
  for (datatype in names(config$platforms[[platform]]$etl)) {
    for (script_def in config$platforms[[platform]]$etl[[datatype]]$scripts) {
      target <- tar_target_raw(
        name = tools::file_path_sans_ext(script_def$script),
        command = substitute(run_etl_script(script), list(script = script_def$script)),
        deps = script_def$depends
      )
      targets_list <- c(targets_list, list(target))
    }
  }

  # Add DRV targets...

  return(targets_list)
}
```

## Benefits of Configuration-Driven Approach

```yaml
configuration_benefits:
  single_source_of_truth:
    - "One file describes entire pipeline"
    - "No scattered dependency information"
    - "Easy to audit and review"

  version_controlled:
    - "Changes tracked in git"
    - "Configuration history available"
    - "Review via pull requests"

  auto_discovery:
    - "New scripts detected automatically"
    - "Reduces manual configuration"
    - "Convention over configuration"

  explicit_when_needed:
    - "Override auto-discovery for special cases"
    - "Clear documentation of exceptions"
    - "Cross-platform dependencies explicit"

  validation:
    - "Catch errors before execution"
    - "Detect orphan scripts"
    - "Verify dependency consistency"
```

## Compliance Checklist

### For Three-Layer Configuration

```yaml
layer_compliance:
  layer_1_universal:
    ✓ Base template at global_scripts/templates/_targets_config.base.yaml
    ✓ Contains schema only (no company settings)
    ✓ Defines phase_order matching DF00
    ✓ Shared via git subrepo (Track 1)

  layer_2_company:
    ✓ pipeline: section in app_config.yaml
    ✓ Contains enabled platforms
    ✓ Contains execution overrides
    ✓ At project root (Track 3)

  layer_3_generated:
    ✓ Output at {project_root}/_targets_config.yaml
    ✓ Generated via make config-merge
    ✓ Valid YAML syntax
    ✓ Committed to version control
```

### For Configuration File

```yaml
config_compliance:
  ✓ Located at {project_root}/_targets_config.yaml
  ✓ Valid YAML syntax
  ✓ Contains version and last_updated
  ✓ Contains template_type: "merged"
  ✓ Defines phase_order matching DF00
  ✓ All platforms have etl and drv sections
  ✓ All scripts have depends defined
  ✓ No circular dependencies
  ✓ Committed to version control
```

### For Pipeline Workflow

```yaml
workflow_compliance:
  ✓ config-merge command available
  ✓ config-scan command available
  ✓ config-full command available
  ✓ config-validate command available
  ✓ Config regenerated when app_config.yaml changes
  ✓ Config committed with code changes
  ✓ CI validates config on PR
```

## Conclusion

The Configuration-Driven Pipeline principle ensures MAMBA pipelines are:

1. **Centralized**: Single configuration file for all pipeline definitions
2. **Discoverable**: Auto-scan directories for new scripts
3. **Explicit**: Special dependencies clearly documented
4. **Validated**: Errors caught before execution
5. **Version-Controlled**: Full history and review capability

**Key Mandates:**
- All pipelines MUST be defined in `_targets_config.yaml`
- Auto-discovery MUST be the default for standard patterns
- Explicit configuration MUST be used for cross-platform and special dependencies
- Configuration MUST be validated before execution
- Configuration changes MUST be committed with code changes
- Platform execution scope MUST be controlled via pipeline config (do not delete shared platform scripts)
